assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements.
The item exposed to foreign exchange risk is an intragroup dividend arrangement denominated in a foreign currency.
The issue is the availability of IFRS hedge accounting for that foreign exchange exposure.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction affecting consolidated profit or loss as a hedged item for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of an intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 specifically allows the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item to qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements when the exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 treat a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk, with related IAS 21 exchange differences accumulated in equity until disposal.
